title: Freeze T556 scope and compatibility boundary
description: Resolve the gap between the task title (LMSR cost function) and the epic wording (per-user asset model transformation), confirm that existing HTTP/event/risk envelopes stay stable during the migration, and lock in market.liquidity as the LMSR b parameter.

title: Define deterministic LMSR account-state shape
description: Specify the internal per-account LMSR-backed state that T556 will persist so later solvency tasks can compute min-assets without reverse-engineering them from legacy scalar fields.

title: Add pure LMSR pricing helpers
description: Introduce dedicated, testable LMSR helpers for liquidity-aware cost calculations and probability-edit transitions instead of continuing to compute order cost directly from KL divergence inside server.py.

title: Persist LMSR-backed account state
description: Extend the account risk/asset storage layer to record the deterministic LMSR-backed state and any compatibility metadata needed to keep current read models functioning until later GS-specific solvency work lands.

title: Wire LMSR cost into ProbabilityEdit
description: Replace the KL-based preview and order-cost path with the new LMSR primitives, thread the new math through unconditional and accepted conditional edits, and preserve idempotency, command/event emission, and current response field names.

title: Refresh math and contract regression coverage
description: Update the affected unit, property, and HTTP integration tests so they assert liquidity-aware LMSR pricing behavior while preserving the existing route, replay, and terminal-event contracts.
